behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: The failure boundary is the real STT API’s diarization output, not just local parsing. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: A mocked transcript with perfect labels would hide real diarization failures; correctness depends on AI/ML output content from STT. MULTI-STEP: Not required to validate this behavior alone, so Workflow is rejected. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Workflow because the behavior text focuses on STT label preservation itself, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real multi-speaker meeting audio sample with known speaker turns, call the real speech-to-text API, and assert returned transcript segments preserve distinguishable speaker labels that can be mapped to attendees.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is parser logic (extract assignee/task text) and can fail within one function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: No OS/network/subprocess or model-quality dependency is required by the behavior text. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration+ because behavior text is limited to deterministic extraction into an input structure, with no required cross-module handoff or external dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed transcript text fixtures covering explicit assignment patterns into the parsing function and assert extracted records contain correct assignee and task text fields in the summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: The key risk is the summarizer omitting action items for some assignees; a stubbed LLM would likely always pass. LLM-DEP: Correctness depends on what the real LLM includes in output. MULTI-STEP: Can be validated with a transcript input plus one real summarization step; full multi-agentic pipeline is not strictly required. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Workflow because behavior text does not require exercising upload/transcribe chain, only live summary coverage per assignee.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a transcript (or transcript-derived summary input) with known task assignments for multiple attendees to the real LLM summarization path, then assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one generated action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is routing/mapping logic after decisions are identified; a local function-level failure is sufficient to reproduce. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: No mandatory real OS/network/model-output dependency in this wording. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration+ because the behavior text specifies deterministic routing into schema fields, not external service behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a structured intermediate object containing identified decisions into the routing logic and assert decisions are emitted under `decisions` schema fields and absent from generic narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: This checks whether the real model extracts and places decision content correctly; deterministic fixtures/stubs hide failures in model judgment and output structure. REAL-INFRA: External API call may be involved, but model-output dependence is the stronger minimum boundary. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Workflow because behavior can be validated with transcript-to-summary generation (single agentic decision stage) without requiring the full multi-stage pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts with known decision statements to the production summarization model path and assert those decisions are present in the generated `decisions` section and not only in freeform narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/INTEGRATION-BRAKE(d): Behavior explicitly requires orchestration across sequential segment processing and merge state handoff. LLM-DEP + MULTI-STEP: Multiple segment-level model calls plus merge-level summarization/aggregation create chained agentic decisions. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Agentic (single-step) because behavior text explicitly requires split -> sequential processing -> merged coherent output, i.e., multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through the production segmentation pipeline, verify segment-by-segment processing order, then assert final merged summary remains coherent and preserves cross-segment action items/decisions without duplication or loss.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: Requires real upload/I/O and service boundaries. LLM-DEP: Includes real transcription/summarization model outputs. MULTI-STEP: Explicit end-to-end chain across multiple stages and handoffs. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text explicitly demands full pipeline sequencing and completion, not an isolated external call.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real meeting audio file through upload, STT, LLM summary generation, and structured output delivery; assert completion status, schema-valid output, and no human intervention/retry steps.
